ground_truth_source: NAS /Volumes/transit (Rush, loaded by evaluate_detection.py default paths)

baseline.notes: Run 43 ceiling-break baseline: F1 0.409, P 0.422, R 0.397, TP 62. After rejecting density (Run 44) and prompt restructure (Run 46) as single-variable levers, Run 47 tests YOLO-driven tight cropping before the VLM. The hypothesis addresses a different root cause: VLM input pixel density, not VLM firing threshold. Smoke-test on 6 frames (3 sporting_ac + 3 Rush) showed dramatic improvement on sporting_ac (sky/mountains/parking removed, players ~2x larger) and neutral-to-positive on Rush (tight crops around goal area, ball zones).

targets.notes: Rush should hold baseline F1 0.409 at minimum. Real prize is cross-game: if Rush F1 holds, immediately queue Run 48 cross-game tests (sporting_ac, reign_wwsurf, reign_rush, capital_fc) with the same YOLO crop config. The cross-game signature to watch: coarse-pass firing rate on sporting_ac should move OFF 3.1% (old 5f baseline) toward Rush's 22.8% range if pixel density was the structural bottleneck.

yolo_crop_enabled.reason: Run #47 feature: soccer-tuned YOLO (yolov8_soccer_uisikdag.pt, classes: ball=0, person=1,2,3) runs on each sampled frame inside the single-pass loop; crops to union(person boxes + ball box) with 10% padding; falls back to full frame if bbox <15% or >85% of frame area. Cropped JPEG is sent to VLM at native crop resolution (not rescaled), so token cost scales with crop size. Integrates with existing YOLO infrastructure from yolo_grounding.py.

notes: Run #47 — YOLO tight-crop pre-pass (commit c230b26). Feature-flagged via YOLO_CROP_ENABLED=true in infra/.env. No other changes vs Run 43. Worker restarted on c230b26 @ 23:23:29Z, verified via runtime config check. Smoke test completed showing clean crops on 6/6 test frames (zero fallbacks, all crops visibly attacking the pixel-density gap).
